title: sagraea sessiliflora
intro: Sagraea sessiliflora, also known as the False Pimpernel, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and its leaves, stems, and roots are used in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Sagraea sessiliflora include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu.
